nodes.speaker: ShipMarket
lines: Heard you can keep a ship in one piece. Mostly.

I hold paper on the old consortium camp — Fiery Twin, trailing edge. Nineteen years abandoned, claim beacons still ticking. Law says the claims transfer to whoever physically retrieves the beacons. Three of them.

nodes.speaker: ShipMarket
lines: Unofficially, friend, there's a reason salvage on that world pays forty percent.

nodes.speaker: ShipMarket
lines: Forty percent of the claim value, plus your pick of any one part off my racks.
Land on the terminator — the line between burn and freeze. The dayside will chew your systems in minutes, so don't be on the dayside.

nodes.speaker: ShipMarket
lines: One more thing. Whatever you find up there — the gear's mine, the paper's mine.
Anything WRITTEN... I don't want anything written. Keep the written things.

responses.buttonText: Why don't you want the written things?

nodes.speaker: ShipMarket
lines: Because I read the first batch, nineteen years ago.

nodes.speaker: ShipMarket
lines: Paper keeps. So does the heat.
